message:
wa-165vw: add proptest suite for TOON serialization roundtrips and token savings

New proptest-based test file covering the TOON (Token-Optimized
Object Notation) integration (wa-165vw) at the `crate::mcp` feature
layer of frankenterm-core. Gated on `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]` — and
therefore only compiled when `mcp` (→ `mcp-server` → `dep:toon_rust`)
is enabled — so default-feature builds don't pull in toon_rust or
proptest just to skip this suite.

Coverage:

* Arbitrary `serde_json::Value` → TOON → back → semantic equality.
* Nested object/array structures survive roundtrip (shrinking-aware
  proptest strategies for leaves and one-level-deep composites).
* Streaming decode matches single-pass decode for the same bytes.
* Token-savings invariant: TOON token count is <= JSON token count
  for non-trivial payloads (the headline wa-165vw claim).
* Canonical form: whitespace/indent variations parse identically.

Strategy design notes baked into the file:

* Numeric leaves are restricted to ±2^53 (f64 safe integer range) and
  the floating-point branch restricts to (0.001 .. 1e6) so proptest
  doesn't have to reason about denormals or NaN round-tripping, which
  TOON does not define a canonical form for.
* String leaves draw from `[a-zA-Z0-9_ ]{0,64}` — enough breadth to
  stress the escape / canonicalization logic without generating
  pathological Unicode corner cases that are covered by unit tests.

All strategies are shrinking-friendly so failures minimize to the
smallest offending payload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
